reasoning: AquaBounty is currently in an out-of-court controlled liquidation. While they faced a severe liquidity crisis at the end of 2024 (cash down to $230k), they successfully completed major asset sales in Feb/Mar 2025, including selling their Canadian farms, corporate IP, and Ohio equipment. These sales raised over $4.1 million in net proceeds and shifted $3.2 million of debt to a buyer, pushing their cash balance up to ~$1.4 million as of late March 2025. The company has ceased farming ops and laid off almost all staff, reducing its burn rate to a minimum. Since the forecast date is May 5, 2025, they only have less than 2 months until the July 1 deadline. With $1.4 million on hand and minimal operating expenses, they have plenty of runway to survive this short period without filing for bankruptcy. Furthermore, they are highly incentivized to preserve their Nasdaq listing (which they have until July 15 to fix via reverse split or merger) and executing a formal bankruptcy would likely destroy that asset. Therefore, a Chapter 11 or 7 filing before July 1 is highly unlikely.
